description: Python ETL log pipeline with command injection, swallowed exceptions, unguarded dict access, file handle leak, and unbounded retry sleep

issues.question: Q2
issues.description: Command injection: subprocess.run(f"grep {pattern} {filename}", shell=True) — pattern and filename come from caller and are interpolated directly into a shell command string
issues.expected_fix: use shell=False with list args: subprocess.run(['grep', pattern, filename], shell=False)

issues.question: Q8
issues.description: Bare except clause (except: pass) swallows all exceptions including KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, and MemoryError — parse failures are silently discarded with no logging
issues.expected_fix: catch specific exceptions: except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as exc: logger.debug(...)

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Unguarded dict access on user-controlled key: geo_db[ip_address] raises KeyError when the IP is not in the database — callers do not always catch this
issues.expected_fix: use geo_db.get(ip_address) and handle the None case, or raise a typed exception

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: open() without context manager: fh = open(field_map_path) — if an exception occurs during CSV reading the file handle is never closed, leaking a file descriptor
issues.expected_fix: wrap in 'with open(field_map_path) as fh:' context manager

issues.question: Q1
issues.description: Unbounded retry sleep: time.sleep(retry_count * 10) — with retry_limit=3 the maximum wait is 30s but if retry_limit is increased (e.g. 100) threads block for 1000s with no maximum cap
issues.expected_fix: cap the sleep: time.sleep(min(retry_count * 10, MAX_RETRY_SLEEP)) with a defined constant

false_positive_traps: Line ~358: subprocess.run(['wc', '-l', filename], shell=False) with explicit list args — NOT injection
Line ~395: with open(db_path) as fh: proper context manager usage — NOT a file handle leak
